sections.heading: Indiana Child Support Calculator: What It Is and How to Use It
sections.content: The Indiana Child Support Calculator is a free online tool that helps parents estimate child support payments according to Indiana state guidelines. By entering income, number of children, custody arrangements, and child-related expenses, it provides a quick and accurate estimate of potential support obligations.

sections.heading: How to Use the Indiana Child Support Calculator

subSections.heading: Enter Parent Income
subSections.content: Input gross weekly or monthly income for each parent.

subSections.heading: Include Deductions
subSections.content: Add prior child support or spousal maintenance payments.

subSections.heading: Specify Number of Children
subSections.content: Enter how many children require support.

subSections.heading: Add Child-Related Expenses
subSections.content: Include childcare costs, health insurance, or other approved expenses.

subSections.heading: Review Results
subSections.content: View base support, adjustments, and total estimated obligation.

subSections.heading: Accurate Indiana Guidelines Calculation
subSections.content: Follows the state’s income shares model.

subSections.heading: Adjustable Inputs
subSections.content: Income, custody schedule, number of children, childcare, and insurance.

subSections.heading: Detailed Breakdown
subSections.content: Base support, adjustments, and total estimated obligation.

subSections.heading: Budget Planning
subSections.content: Know expected monthly or weekly obligations.

subSections.heading: Divorce & Separation Prep
subSections.content: Negotiate agreements or anticipate court rulings.

subSections.heading: Legal Guidance
subSections.content: Visualize obligations for lawyers or mediators.

subSections.heading: Custody Planning
subSections.content: Compare scenarios and see how support changes.

subSections.heading: Financial Clarity
subSections.content: Understand how support is calculated in Indiana.

subSections.heading: Example 1 – Single Parent
subSections.content: Parent A Income: $1,200/week | Parent B Income: $800/week | Children: 2 | Childcare/Health Expenses: $100/week → Estimated Weekly Support: $350

subSections.heading: Example 2 – Shared Custody
subSections.content: Parent A Income: $1,500/week | Parent B Income: $1,000/week | Children: 1 | Shared Custody: 3 overnights/week for Parent B → Estimated Weekly Support: $220

faq.q: What is the Indiana Child Support Calculator?
faq.a: It estimates child support obligations based on Indiana state guidelines.
No, it works in any browser.

faq.q: Does it calculate exact court orders?
faq.a: No, final amounts may differ at the judge’s discretion.

faq.q: Can it include childcare and insurance costs?
faq.a: Yes, you can input these expenses.

faq.q: Does it account for custody time?

faq.a: Yes, the number of children is included in the calculation.

faq.q: Is it suitable for legal planning?
faq.a: Yes, but it’s not a substitute for professional legal advice.

faq.q: Can incomes be adjusted for deductions?
faq.a: Yes, prior obligations like existing support or spousal maintenance can be added.
Results are accurate based on the inputs, but court-ordered support may vary.
